t_ch: 堂堂「共愿帮」，既有胆量请动「仙舟第一杀手」……
t_en: So, since the mighty Synwish Syndicate gang actually had the guts to hire the Xianzhou's Number One Assassin...

t_ch: 想来，也做好了「毁灭」的准备吧？
t_en: I guess you are also ready for "destruction."

t_ch: （多年奔走空皮骨，方知人间行路难……）
t_en: (Years of wandering have given me nothing but exhaustion, and I have finally come to know how harsh the road of life truly is...)

t_ch: （「冒充仙舟第一杀手」…爻老板，你倒是给小女子挑了件「好差事」啊。）
t_en: ("Impersonating the Xianzhou's Number One Assassin"... Madam Yao, you really stuck me with a "wonderful task.")

t_ch: 不久前……
t_en: Not long ago...

t_ch: 此番行动，停云姑娘还请务必注意安全。要是有个三长两短，我实在不好向你家驭空司舵交代。
t_en: Please take great care during this mission, Miss Tingyun. If any misfortune should befall you, I would have no way to answer to your Helm Master Yukong.

t_ch: 爻老板多虑了。在向驭空大人言明心志、动身启行「玉阙」时，停云便已做好了最坏的准备。
t_en: You worry too much, Madam Yao. When I pledged my resolve to Madam Yukong and departed for the Yuque, I had already steeled myself for the worst.

t_ch: 只是…容小女子多问一句：这趟「三长两短」的行动，非得是我么？
t_en: But... may I ask: Does this task of "misfortune" really have to fall upon me?

t_ch: 我今日算过一卦，这活儿命里缺火，少了鸣火商团接渡使的八面玲珑，可没法成事。
t_en: I cast a divination today. This task lacks the fire element. Without the Whistling Flames Amicassador's knack for diplomacy, this task cannot succeed.

t_ch: 你要去的地方盘踞着不少帮派分子，若是路遇挑衅，不妨抬出「仙舟第一杀手」的名头，也许能让他们自动退避。
t_en: The place you're heading to is crawling with gang members. If they pick a fight, you can try to drop the name "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin" and they might step aside.

t_ch: 将军不必担心，多年来我随商团走南闯北，也没少踏进过危险的地方。区区帮派，倒也不算难打交道。
t_en: Do not worry, general. I have journeyed far and wide with the merchant guilds for years and faced no shortage of danger. A handful of gangsters is hardly difficult to deal with.

t_ch: 既然不怕危险，怎么迟迟不动身？要不要我为你占上一卦，喂你吃颗定心丸？
t_en: If you're not afraid of danger, then why have you not set out yet? Shall I read your fortune and give you a little peace of mind?

t_ch: 小女子怕的正是您「占上一卦」。不，应该说，小女子怕的是爻老板您啊。
t_en: What I fear is that "fortune-telling" of yours... No, what I really fear is you, Madam Yao.

t_ch: 怕我？我在罗浮仙舟就这么声名狼藉吗？
t_en: Afraid of me? Am I so notorious on the Xianzhou Luofu?

t_ch: 我听过不少您的逸话——打从方壶一战后您自愿交出了符节，甘当无兵无卒的「文职将军」。他们还说……
t_en: I've heard many tales about you. After the Battle of the Fanghu, you willingly surrendered your command token and became a "general in name only," with neither troops nor soldiers. They also say...

t_ch: 戎韬将军为了谋得半成胜机，坐视{RUBY_B#玉阙太卜}自己的恩师{RUBY_E#}赴死成仁，将青丘军万千军士的性命当作卜算中无谓消耗的数字，<color=#dbc291ff>甚至还「算死」了一位</color>{RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>月御</color>}<color=#dbc291ff>帝弓天将</color>{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: That the Seer Strategist, for the sake of a tiny chance at victory, stood by as {RUBY_B#the Yuque Master Diviner}her mentor{RUBY_E#} went to a noble death, treated the countless lives of dead Verdant Knights as nothing more than numbers consumed in her divinations, and <color=#dbc291ff>even "doomed" an</color> {RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>Yueyu</color>}<color=#dbc291ff>Arbiter-General</color>{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 你是担心我这个「冷血卜者」，将你「算死」在这二相乐园？你可真敢说啊，小狐狸！
t_en: Are you afraid that I, the "cold-blooded diviner," might send you to your "doom" here in Planarcadia? Bold words for a little fox!

t_ch: 不过是对将军的一点小小好奇心，还请将军解我心中疑惑一二。
t_en: It's just a bit of my curiosity about you, general. I hoped you could clear up a few of my doubts.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我确实没看错人。面对仙舟将军，少有人能做到这般不卑不亢。
t_en: Hahaha, seems like I wasn't wrong about you. Few can face a Xianzhou general with such composure and dignity.

t_ch: 也难怪你能说出这番豪言——「天上煌煌众神以人为弈，博戏群星，留下一地冤魂孤冢、白骨累累…您可曾想过，要让祂们付出代价？」
t_en: No wonder you could make such a bold statement: "The gods use humans as pawns in their cosmic game, sacrificing countless innocent souls... Have you ever thought about making THEM pay?"

t_ch: 他们都说你从绝灭大君魔爪下死里逃生，是靠福缘深厚；要我说，靠的是你这份难以摧折的韧性才对。
t_en: They say you survived a Lord Ravager's deadly clutches thanks to luck, but I believe it was your unbreakable resilience that carried you through.

t_ch: 爻老板谬赞了。总得收获些道理，才对得起自己经历的这番生死劫数呀。
t_en: You flatter me, Madam Yao. One must glean some wisdom to be worthy of enduring such a life-and-death ordeal.

t_ch: 倘若只在冷血无情上胜过众神，在小女子看来，反倒是一败涂地呢。
t_en: If we surpass the gods only in cruelty and heartlessness, then that would be our complete failure in my eyes.

t_ch: …不错，甚合我心！停云姑娘——你通过面试了。
t_en: ...Excellent, I couldn't agree more! Miss Tingyun, you've passed the interview.

t_ch: 打从死里逃生后，我便下定决心，不为无情无心的棋手所驱遣。爻老板，您也通过了小女子的面试。
t_en: Ever since I escaped death, I swore never again to be a pawn for heartless chess players. And Madam Yao, you've passed my test, too.

t_ch: 我知道你对虚的不感兴趣，那就来谈些实际的吧。停云姑娘，我们玉阙的使团因公出行时，待遇可比罗浮好多了！一日五餐——
t_en: I know you're not interested in empty talk, so let's get down to business. Miss Tingyun, our Yuque business delegations are treated much better than what you get on the Luofu when they set out on official missions! They get five meals a day...

t_ch: 将军，小女子实在没那么大的胃口。
t_en: General, I really don't have such a large appetite.

t_ch: 随使团行动期间津贴六倍，花费一律报销。
t_en: For the duration of the delegation's mission, you'll receive six times the usual allowance, and all expenses will be fully reimbursed.

t_ch: 报酬这么优厚？实不相瞒，这倒更让我惶恐了——
t_en: Such a generous offer? To be honest, that makes me even more nervous...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>前往鸽川区，找到名为「共愿帮」的帮派接头人，用您提供给我的暗号接头，不得暴露真名。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Head to Dovebrook District, locate the Synwish Syndicate contact, use the passphrase you gave me to establish contact, and do not reveal my true name.</color>

t_ch: 若是没有其他隐情，这事值得六倍厚报？
t_en: If there's nothing more to it, is this task really worth six times the pay?

t_ch: 你是值得这个报酬的人才。说起来，我们卜者和你们行商这行并无差别。纵然遇上山穷水尽的局面，也要勉力讨价还价，凿开一线机会。
t_en: You're the kind of talent worthy of such a reward. After all, we diviners are no different from you merchants. Even when we hit a dead end, we still have to bargain and carve out the faintest chance.

t_ch: 行商争的是得利的机会，而卜者争的是一线天机。
t_en: Merchants strive for profitable opportunities, while diviners reach for a sliver of luck.

t_ch: 看来，我这趟行动在爻老板占卜的命数中颇为重要。那么，我就出发了。
t_en: Looks like my mission bears significant weight in Madam Yao's reading of fate. In that case, I shall depart.

t_ch: 遇事不决时，你可以打开我交给你的三枚锦囊，其中书写着我占卜得来的应对方略，一定能解开困局，对你的行动大有裨益。
t_en: When in doubt, open the three strategy pouches I entrusted to you. Inside are tactics I divined, certain to break any deadlock and aid your journey.

t_ch: 去吧，「仙舟第一杀手」。
t_en: Go, "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin."

t_ch: 请告诉老大，我要回到共愿帮！
t_en: Please tell the boss I want to return to the Synwish Syndicate!

t_ch: …大姐，还是早点安息吧？你不记得自己在几周前行动的时候死了吗？现在的「你」，只是你死前留下的一缕执念啊。
t_en: ...Ma'am, maybe it's time to rest in peace? Don't you remember dying during that mission a few weeks ago? The "you" right now is just a lingering obsession left behind after death.

t_ch: 按照《灵物保护法》的相关法规规定，幽灵不等同于生前个体。换句话说，你只是个刚满三个月的新生幻造种。
t_en: According to the provisions of the Faekind Protection Act, ghosts are not considered the same as they were while alive. In other words, you are a new imagenae, barely three months old.

t_ch: 你到底是律师还是帮派弟兄啊？我可是为了共愿帮而死的，你们现在却要把我拒之门外？
t_en: Are you a lawyer or a gangster? I gave my life for the Synwish Syndicate, and now you mean to shut me out?

t_ch: （这两人…是共愿帮的成员？）
t_en: (These two... are members of the Synwish Syndicate?)

t_ch: 你听不明白吗？过去的你，死了，连犯罪记录也一笔勾销了。老大的意思是，有洗白上岸的机会，就别再来混帮派啦——
t_en: Don't you get it? The old you's dead, and even your criminal record got wiped clean. The leader's tryin'a say that now you've got a chance to keep to the straight an' narrow. You shouldn't come back to the gang...

t_ch: 我该怎么解释你才…喂喂，这位狐妖小姐，你在一旁偷听？
t_en: How am I supposed to convince you... Hey, fox girl, you eavesdroppin' over there?

t_ch: 我只是个来谈生意的仙舟人，请问共愿帮该往哪个方向去？
t_en: I'm just a Xianzhou person here to talk business. Could you tell me which way to the Synwish Syndicate?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「我从仙舟来，来谈笔买卖，消磨自身、撕碎敌人的买卖。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"I come from the Xianzhou to discuss a deal: A deal that wears me down, yet tears my enemies apart."</color>

t_ch: …做生意？仙舟人？
t_en: ...Business? From the Xianzhou?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>共愿帮的总部…就、就在河的对岸。</color>请您一路走好。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The Synwish Syndicate office... is j—just across the river.</color> Have a safe journey.

t_ch: （他不像是听懂了暗号…但请来「仙舟第一杀手」的消息倒是组织上下都传遍了。）
t_en: (He doesn't seem to have understood the passphrase... but the news that "the Xianzhou's Number One Assassin" is coming has already spread through the organization.)

t_ch: 共愿帮的总部…就、就在河的对岸。请您一路走好。
t_en: The Synwish Syndicate office... is j—just across the river. Have a safe journey.

t_ch: 这位狐狸小姐，您是外星来的旅客？请问您有兴趣了解一下我们「幸福微笑研究会」吗？
t_en: Miss fox, are you a visitor from another planet? Would you be interested in learning about our Research Society of Happy Smiles?

t_ch: 瞧这说法，怕是要推销？
t_en: Hmm, sounds like a sales pitch?

t_ch: 推销？不不不。我们只是想送您一份礼物——发自内心的幸福笑容。
t_en: Sales pitch? No, no, no. We just want to give you a gift. A genuine smile of happiness straight from the heart.

t_ch: 真正的笑容是由内向外焕发的幸福力量。我们「幸福微笑研究会」可以给您带来真正的幸福感。「幸福和欢笑~从今天开始！」
t_en: A genuine smile is the power of happiness shining from within. Our Research Society of Happy Smiles can grant you real joy. "Happiness and laughter~ starting today!"

t_ch: 现在加入，我们将赠送您《关于人生幸福的黄金准则》系列书目和满愿电视台会员资格——可以免费收看全年节目哦~
t_en: Join now and receive our Golden Rules of Life's Happiness book series along with a Fulwish TV membership. You can watch every program free for a whole year~

t_ch: （噗，这不还是推销吗？被同行缠上可要耽误时间了。不如，让她知难而退吧。）
t_en: (Pfft, isn't this just another sales pitch? Getting tangled up with a fellow merchant will only waste my time. Better to make her back off.)

t_ch: 实在抱歉，我来这儿是见共愿帮的。
t_en: I'm sorry, but I am here to meet with the Synwish Syndicate.

t_ch: 真遗憾…如果未来您需要「幸福微笑研究会」的话，请随时联系我。
t_en: What a pity... If you ever need the Research Society of Happy Smiles, please reach out to me anytime.

t_ch: 女人带着幸福的微笑递上名片，鞠了一躬，不再说话。
t_en: The lady handed over her business card with a blissful smile, bowed, and said nothing more.

t_ch: 共愿帮…像是美丽的鸽川河道上最碍眼的垃圾啊。
t_en: The Synwish Syndicate... is like the most disgusting filth staining the beautiful Dovebrook River.

t_ch: 是呢是呢，要是能让他们全都消失就好啦。「幸福和欢笑~从今天开始！」
t_en: Yeah. If only we could make them all disappear. "Happiness and laughter~ starting today!"

t_ch: 大哥…咱们到底还要等多久啊？
t_en: Bro... how much longer do we have to wait?

t_ch: 怎么，腿酸脚软了？笨蛋！老大让我们在这儿候着表示尊重！你们给我安分点！
t_en: What, your legs are giving out? Idiot! The boss told us to wait here as a show of respect. Now behave yourselves!

t_ch: （那名壮汉…会是共愿帮的接头人吗？）
t_en: (Could that big guy... be the contact from the Synwish Syndicate?)

t_ch: 那边那只狐狸！打刚才起就对咱们弟兄几个瞅个不停，你有什么意见？
t_en: Hey, you fox over there! You've been staring at us long enough. Got something to say?

t_ch: 敢问，前面可是共愿帮的宝地？
t_en: May I ask, is this the Synwish Syndicate's turf up ahead?

t_ch: 是又怎么着？
t_en: Yeah, so what?

t_ch: （见面先高三分嗓，手里有活也不多……）
t_en: (I should act tough, and say less than what I want to say...)

t_ch: 既然前方就是共愿帮的地头…小女子我非但要进，还得您恭恭敬敬请我进去——
t_en: Since this is the Synwish Syndicate's turf ahead... not only am I going in, you will invite me in with respect...

t_ch: 你说什么？我看你是来闹事的吧？！
t_en: What did you say? You're here to stir up trouble, aren't you!?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>「我从仙舟来，来谈笔买卖，消磨自身、撕碎敌人的买卖。」</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>"I come from the Xianzhou to discuss a deal: A deal that wears me down, yet tears my enemies apart."</color>

t_ch: …你是老大请来的客人！？
t_en: ...You're the guest our boss invited!?

t_ch: 不对！差点给你混过去，就你这娇滴滴的样子，能是「仙舟第一杀手」？
t_en: Hold on! I nearly fell for that. Looking all delicate... how could you possibly be the Xianzhou's Number One Assassin?

t_ch: 说！你从哪儿知道的暗号？
t_en: Spill it! How did you know the passphrase?

t_ch: 要不，您先把墨镜摘了，让小女子瞧瞧…您的眼睛是不是还在？
t_en: Why don't you take off your sunglasses and let me see if you still have your eyes?

t_ch: 「仙舟第一杀手」…不过是俗人送的俗名罢了。小女子一直自认是个生意人。
t_en: "The Xianzhou's Number One Assassin"... is nothing more than a nickname coined by the common folk. I have always considered myself a merchant.

t_ch: 生意人？
t_en: A merchant?

t_ch: 杀人，不就是这世上最古老的生意么？
t_en: Isn't a hired killer the oldest trade in the world?

t_ch: 对了，幻造种先生，你可知道我为何会被夸称「第一」么？
t_en: By the way, Mr. Imagenae... do you know why they call me "Number One"?

t_ch: …你从不失手？
t_en: ...you never miss a shot?

t_ch: 不对。我「杀得多」。懂得让利，生意才做得长久——雇主指名要杀一个，我就帮他多杀一双。
t_en: Not quite. I "kill in bulk." The way to keep business going is to offer a little extra. If they pay for one person to be silenced, I throw in two more.

t_ch: 你这动辄找事的火爆性子，应该没少给贵帮{RUBY_B#老大}龙头{RUBY_E#}惹麻烦吧？把你当作「赠品」，想来他也不会有意见。
t_en: With that hot temper of yours, always picking fights... you've probably given your boss plenty of headaches. I doubt the boss would mind if I took you as a "bonus kill."

t_ch: 你这家伙…对二当家好无礼！
t_en: You punk... How dare you disrespect the Underboss!

t_ch: 都给我闭嘴！老大交代的正事要紧！
t_en: Shut up, all of you! The boss's orders come first!

t_ch: 我叫恶水——刚才弟兄几个失礼了，晚些切根手指给您赔罪！
t_en: My name is Caustide. Sorry about my underlings' rudeness earlier. I'll cut a finger off later to make amends!

t_ch: 那倒也不必，我生平只要首级，不要指头。
t_en: There's no need for that. I only collect heads, not fingers.

t_ch: 不妨事，反正不切我的。
t_en: No worries. It won't be my finger anyway.

t_ch: （这些莽夫应该不难拿下…他们从未见过「仙舟第一杀手」的容貌，这便好办多了。）
t_en: (These brutes should be easy to handle... They've never seen the face of "the Xianzhou's Number One Assassin," so this'll be much simpler.)

t_ch: 小弟该怎么称呼您？总不能一直大呼小叫杀手、杀手来着？
t_en: What should I call you? Yelling "assassin, assassin" every time just won't cut it.

t_ch: …就叫小女子「不归人」吧。
t_en: ...Just call me "The Tarrying Fugue."

t_ch: 怖…鬼人？霸气侧漏，霸气侧漏啊！
t_en: The Terror... in Fog? That name oozes menace... completely overwhelming!

t_ch: 现在，可以带路了吧？
t_en: Now then, can you lead the way?

t_ch: 这里应该和爻老板的任务无关，去别处看看吧？
t_en: This probably has nothing to do with Madam Yao's mission. I should look somewhere else.

t_ch: 怖鬼大人您走错方向了，前方才是咱们的总部。
t_en: Lady Terror in Fog, you went the wrong way. Our headquarters are straight ahead.

t_ch: 二当家，我绝对没有质疑老大的意思，但是…这个柔弱的女人真的是老大请来的人吗？！
t_en: Underboss, I mean no disrespect, but... is this frail woman really the one the boss invited!?

t_ch: 对！这位小姐名叫「怖•鬼人」——给我把消息放出去，就说咱们共愿帮请到了「仙舟第一杀手」！
t_en: That's right! Her name is The Terror in Fog. Spread the word that the Synwish Syndicate has secured Xianzhou's Number One Assassin!

t_ch: 二当家，就算被打我也要说——认真的吗？我们真的要靠这样一个娇滴滴的女人去参加「幻月游戏」？
t_en: Underboss, beat me if you want, but I gotta say... Are you serious? Are we really counting on this delicate woman for the Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: （「幻月游戏」…他们是为此才请来星核猎手捉刀代行？）
t_en: (The Phantasmoon Games... Is that why they hired a Stellaron Hunter to do their bidding?)

t_ch: 这次「幻月游戏」，可是咱们共愿帮难逢的崛起机会啊！这女人看上去不像有两把刷子，托付给她？要是办砸了，咱们这些弟兄面子往哪儿搁？
t_en: These Phantasmoon Games are a once-in-a-lifetime chance for the Synwish Syndicate to rise! This woman doesn't look like she has what it takes, and we're trusting her? If she blows it, how are we supposed to save face?

t_ch: 二当家！咱们不是被骗了吧？这要传出去可是要被别的帮派笑话的——
t_en: Underboss! We haven't been duped, have we? If word gets out, the other gangs will laugh us out of town...

t_ch: <size=28>统统都给我闭嘴！</size>
t_en: <size=28>All of you, shut up!</size>

t_ch: 你们这帮饭桶，怖鬼大人动起手来，你们一块上也不够用！既然你们怀疑，不如就让怖鬼大人给你们露两手！
t_en: Useless! Lady Terror in Fog would wipe the floor with you even if you all came at once. Since you doubt her, why not let Lady Terror in Fog show you a few moves?

t_ch: 怖鬼大人，您看怎么样？我给您找几个陪练的，不用留手。
t_en: Lady Terror in Fog, what do you say? I can find you a few sparring partners. No need to hold back.

t_ch: 我还道你是心思单纯，合着…是在玩些红脸白脸的手段，想来试试小女子？
t_en: I thought you were naive, but looks like you're just playing good cop, bad cop to test me?

t_ch: 哪会，我脸一直都是黄的。
t_en: Nah, I'm neither good nor bad... just opportunistic.

t_ch: 还在装疯卖傻……
t_en: Still playing dumb, huh...

t_ch: 共愿帮的当家，你这些手下想来不是擅作主张，你一定听得见——
t_en: Boss of the Synwish Syndicate, your people wouldn't be doing this on their own. I know you can hear me...

t_ch: 小女子是来做生意的，若能交些朋友，更是一桩美事。可若是交不到，也没关系。
t_en: I came for business. Making friends would be a fine bonus, but if not, that's fine with me.

t_ch: 你若还想做这笔生意，就别再玩这些手段了。
t_en: If you still want this deal, cut the tricks.

t_ch: 高手就是不一样，说起话来霸气侧漏——就是听不懂。您就露一手吧，兄弟们天天打打杀杀，非得这样才能服气。
t_en: A real expert is something else. You speak with such overwhelming presence... though we barely understand a word. Come on, show us a move. We fight and bleed every day, and that's the only way we'll be convinced.

t_ch: 麻烦，遇上了些不想两全其美的莽汉呐……
t_en: Oh no. These brutes are not interested in a win–win situation at all...

t_ch: 「遇事不决时，你可以打开我交给你的三枚锦囊，其中书写着我占卜得来的应对方略，一定能解开困局，对你的行动大有裨益。」
t_en: "When in doubt, open the three strategy pouches I entrusted to you. Inside are tactics I divined, certain to break any deadlock and aid your journey."

t_ch: 怕是到时候了呀——
t_en: Looks like it's time...
Huh...

t_ch: 「别死就行。完事了再打开第二个锦囊。(^O^)/」
t_en: "Just keep... alive, and open the second strategy pouch once you're done. (^O^)/"

t_ch: （这叫什么锦囊，信息量真是低得令人发指。）
t_en: (What kind of strategy is this? There's barely anything helpful in here.)

t_ch: 看来怖鬼大人瞧不上你们的本事——都让开！
t_en: Looks like Lady Terror in Fog isn't impressed with your skills. Outta my way!

t_ch: 鄙人小时候曾怀揣音乐梦想。无奈命运弄人，我最终踏入了刀光剑影的世界。
t_en: When I was young, I dreamed of becoming a musician. But fate had other plans, and I was dragged into a world of violence.

t_ch: 但即便如此，我也未曾放下对音乐和节奏的热爱。就算在战斗时，我也时刻苦练着歌艺和演奏技巧！
t_en: But even then, I never let go of my love for music and rhythm. Even in battle, I kept honing my singing and instrumental skills!

t_ch: 这也成就了我独一无二的战斗风格——人称「咆哮的恶水」！
t_en: And that's how I forged my one-of-a-kind fighting style. They call me... the Roaring Caustide!

t_ch: 糟糕，二当家…这是要动真格了啊！
t_en: Oh cribs! The Underboss... is getting serious!

t_ch: 怖鬼大人，请接受鄙人的挑战！向我们共愿帮展示您引以为豪的杀人之术吧！
t_en: Lady Terror in Fog, I humbly challenge you! Show the Synwish Syndicate the assassination skills you're so proud of!

t_ch: 二当家，不好啦！又有两个怪人出现了！
t_en: Underboss! Bad news! Two more weird dudes just showed up!

t_ch: 废话，我长着眼睛呢！
t_en: Cut your bull. I can see for myself!

t_ch: 我确实没想到，拜会盟友，竟然变成了硬闯当地帮派的会堂。
t_en: Never thought visiting an ally would mean crashing into a local gang's hall.

t_ch: 两位，此事说来话长……
t_en: Ah, this will take some explaining...

t_ch: 真是阴差阳错，但能在这见到一位老朋友…也罢。
t_en: What a twist of fate... but for us to reunite with an old friend here... so be it.

t_ch: 你们两个，不许插手！给我听好了！这是一场堂堂正正的决斗！
t_en: You two, stand down! Listen well! This will be an honorable duel!

t_ch: 决斗？你对一位仙舟——
t_en: A duel? You dare duel a Xianzh—

t_ch: …一位仙舟第一杀手，你们还敢如此放肆？
t_en: ...a Xianzhou's Number One Assassin, and you still dare to act so brazen?

t_ch: 等等，「一位」？什么叫「一位」仙舟第一杀手？你们几个…到底是什么来头？！
t_en: Wait, "a"? What do you mean, "a" Xianzhou's Number One Assassin? Who exactly are you!?

t_ch: 狡兔尚且三窟，是谁和你说…「仙舟第一杀手」只有一人？
t_en: Even a cunning hare keeps three burrows. Who ever told you... there was only one "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin"?

t_ch: 所以…是三个人？
t_en: So... there are three of you?

t_ch: 是三百个！时移世易，「仙舟第一杀手」早已品牌化了。我身边这一位……
t_en: No! It's three hundred! Times have changed. The title "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin" is already a brand. At my side stands...

t_ch: 正是组织内排名第七的天环杀手，人称「报丧鸟」！他会给所有目标带来甜梦般的安眠。
t_en: The organization's seventh-ranked Halovian Reaper, the "Winged Deathknell"! He sends every target into the sweetest sleep of death.

t_ch: 而这位，则是组织内排名第二，仅在我之后的无情刺客！枪如流星人如龙，人称「玉面龙」！
t_en: And this one is the second-ranked assassin in the organization, a ruthless killer surpassed only by me! His spear strikes like a falling star, and his presence soars like a dragon. They call him the "Jadedragon"!

t_ch: 那你又是……？
t_en: Then you are...?

t_ch: 你可以称我「鸣火狐」。小女子忝列组织内第一把交椅，不过平日里只做些商务接洽和人力资源的工作。
t_en: You can call me "Whistling Flamefox." I humbly hold the top position in our organization, though I mostly handle business relations and HR work.

t_ch: 听起来有点难以置信，是吗？
t_en: Hard to believe, isn't it?

t_ch: 大姐，事到如今，说什么都没有意义啦。
t_en: Come on, Madam, it's pointless to say anything now.

t_ch: 为了让眼前这位先生了解您所言非虚，还是让{RUBY_B#丹恒}二哥{RUBY_E#}露一两手枪术，也好让这些人心服口服。
t_en: If you want this gentleman to know you speak the truth, let {RUBY_B#Dan Heng}our brother{RUBY_E#} show off a bit of his spear work. That will convince them.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我看那就不必了吧！
t_en: Hahaha, I say there's no need for that!

t_ch: 恶水，你小子非要试试成色，没讨着好吧？早点放人家进来和我见面不就没事了？
t_en: Caustide, you insisted on testing them and got nothing out of it, did you? If you had let them meet me sooner, none of this would have happened.

t_ch: 老大！我可不能把您的大业随便托付给不知底细的人！
t_en: Boss! I cannot hand your grand enterprise over to people we know nothing about!

t_ch: 您就是雇佣我们的共愿帮老大？
t_en: You are the one who hired us? The boss of the Synwish Syndicate?

t_ch: 是我。我叫池波，共愿帮这一代的头领。实在抱歉，帮里都是些不懂事的大老粗，过后我会好好管教。
t_en: That's me. I am Polwave, current leader of the Synwish Syndicate. My apologies. Our gang is full of roughnecks. I will make sure they are disciplined.

t_ch: 关于「那笔生意」，我们在楼上详谈吧。其他人，统统散了吧！
t_en: As for "that business," we'll discuss it upstairs. Everyone else, clear out!

t_ch: 久违了，停云女士，匹诺康尼一别也有些时日了。
t_en: Long time no see, Lady Tingyun. It's been a while since we last met in Penacony.

t_ch: 两位能临机应变，小女子感激不尽。为免暴露更多信息，刚才胡乱称呼，得罪之处还请见谅。
t_en: I am truly grateful for your quick thinking. To avoid revealing too much, I made up false names and titles for you two just now... But if you were offended, then I beg your forgiveness.

t_ch: 这儿堪称是非之地，两位怎么来这儿了？
t_en: This place is a hotbed of trouble. What brings you two here?

t_ch: 我和星期日与仙舟的使团搭上线时，收到他们的请求，说使团中有人异乡迷途，不慎陷落当地帮派之手，希望我们伸出援手。
t_en: When Sunday and I contacted the Xianzhou delegation, they asked for our help. They claimed someone from their group had lost their way here in a foreign land and was captured by a local gang, and they wanted us to help.

t_ch: 虽说一听就是另有隐情，但我们还是来了。
t_en: It was clear there was more to the story, but we came anyway.

t_ch: 谁知道…竟然确有其事。
t_en: Who would've thought... it turned out to be true.

t_ch: 「迷途陷落」？当中有些误会——唉，两位这则流言，恐怕是爻老板精心安排的「命数」吧。
t_en: "Lost"? "Captured"? There must be some misunderstanding... Alas, this rumor was likely a "destiny" arranged by Madam Yao.

t_ch: 你刚才提到「爻老板」，莫非是玉阙仙舟的戎韬将军？
t_en: Did you just say "Madam Yao"? Do you mean the Seer Strategist from the Xianzhou Yuque?

t_ch: 我在匹诺康尼时也偶有耳闻，听说她是位深居简出的卜者将军。这次，联盟派了她前来？
t_en: I happened to hear of her back in Penacony too. They say she is the diviner general, one who lives in seclusion. Did the Alliance send her this time?

t_ch: 停云女士亲涉险地，莫非是要务在身？
t_en: Lady Tingyun, you stepped into such danger yourself... do you have a vital mission?

t_ch: 两位不是外人，我就不瞒二位了，就是她差我来此执行任务。
t_en: You two are no strangers... so I will speak plainly. She's the one who sent me here on this mission.

t_ch: 共愿帮请来「仙舟第一杀手」，想雇佣这位凶名卓著的人物参与「幻月游戏」。但按使团所获情报，那人的真实身份是星核猎手，刃。
t_en: The Synwish Syndicate invited the notorious "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin" with the intention of hiring this person to participate in the Phantasmoon Games. But the delegation's intelligence revealed that the person was none other than the Stellaron Hunter, Blade.

t_ch: …是他？
t_en: ...Him?

t_ch: 我冒用这一名号前来此地，就是为了探明星核猎手为何，以及如何入局。
t_en: I assumed the title to come here to uncover why the Stellaron Hunters are involved... and how he will enter the game.

t_ch: 眼下进展如何？
t_en: So how's it going so far?

t_ch: 虽说阴差阳错之下，一切都很顺利，但接下来该如何做，小女子也还蒙在鼓里。
t_en: By sheer twists of fate, everything has gone smoothly so far. But as for what comes next... I still don't know anything either.

t_ch: 不过，临行前爻老板曾交予我三只锦囊，内藏妙计…嗯，姑且先当作妙计吧。眼下，也到了拆开第二只的时候。
t_en: Still, Madam Yao entrusted me with three pouches before I departed, each said to hold a stratagem. Well... let us call them stratagems for now. And at this moment, it is time to open the second.

t_ch: …锦囊？
t_en: ...A strategy pouch?

t_ch: 「你还是说出我的身份了吗？无妨！我给你派来援手咯，想怎么用都可以，第三个见了共愿帮头目再拆！b（￣▽￣）」
t_en: "So you revealed my identity after all? No worries! I've sent you some backup. Use them however you like. Open the third pouch only after you've met the Synwish Syndicate's boss. b（￣▽￣）"

t_ch: 爻老板，难怪您也有「相声将军」的名头呐……
t_en: No wonder they also call Madam Yao the "Crosstalk General"...

t_ch: 怎么说？
t_en: The what general?

t_ch: 上面说，两位会为我解围，让我安心去见共愿帮的头目——两位意下如何？
t_en: It says the two of you will help me clear the way, so I can safely meet the Synwish Syndicate's boss. What do you say?

t_ch: 我们……
t_en: We...

t_ch: 这里万分凶险，我们当然会与停云小姐随行。
t_en: This place is perilous. Of course we will accompany you, Miss Tingyun.

t_ch: 至于星核猎手，我同样对他们很感兴趣。
t_en: As for the Stellaron Hunters... I, too, find them intriguing.

t_ch: 丹恒，能再陪我打听些消息吗？
t_en: Dan Heng, will you help me gather a bit more information?

t_ch: 当然…谢谢你，星期日。
t_en: Of course... thank you, Sunday.

t_ch: 一不留神，鸽川的风景就变得面目全非了。
t_en: Before I even realized, the scenery of Dovebrook became unrecognizable.

t_ch: 以前哪儿会有{RUBY_B#机铠}铁坨子{RUBY_E#}满街跑呢？更别提把我们这些帮派分子当成害虫处理了。
t_en: Back then, who would have imagined {RUBY_B#mechatrons}iron hulks{RUBY_E#} running wild on the streets, let alone us gang members being treated like vermin?

t_ch: 您找我们来，显然不是来怀旧的吧？
t_en: You didn't call us here just to reminisce, did you?

t_ch: 哈哈，当然不是！三位，请原谅恶水这家伙无礼的试探行为。
t_en: Haha, of course not! Please forgive Caustide's rude little test.

t_ch: 这只井底之蛙，总是搞不明白年轻人为什么喜欢从银河里传来的东西。机铠、蒙面英雄、星穹列车……
t_en: That narrow-minded fellow never understands why young people flock to things that come from other planets, like mechatrons, masked heroes, and the Astral Express...

t_ch: 他不明白，<color=#dbc291ff>银河里来的东西更新鲜，银河里来的大人物也更可怕。</color>
t_en: What he does not see is that <color=#dbc291ff>while cosmic novelties are more enticing, big shots that hail from the cosmos are even more fearsome.</color>

t_ch: 几位，我说的没错吧？
t_en: Wouldn't you agree?

t_ch: 您是聪明人，再装下去不过是自讨没趣。我们并非「仙舟第一杀手」，这点您应该心知肚明？
t_en: You're a clever one, so there's no point in keeping up this charade. We're not "Xianzhou's Number One Assassins." You knew that already, didn't you?

t_ch: 我虽然不像仙舟人那样长寿，但好歹有些看人的眼光。你们不是第一杀手，但本领胜似第一杀手。
t_en: I may not live as long as those Xianzhou folks, but I've got a decent eye for sizing people up. You might not be the number one assassins, but your skills surpass even such assassins.

t_ch: 所以，我不在乎你们是谁。抛出大价钱请来了「快刀」，又何必管它是什么牌子？
t_en: So I don't care who you are. I paid good money for a "sharp blade"...but I don't give a fig about its brand.

t_ch: 那这把快刀，您是想用在哪儿？
t_en: And where do you intend to use this blade?

t_ch: 共愿帮。
t_en: On the Synwish Syndicate.

t_ch: 什么？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 别误会，我不是想看到血流成河，而是想给共愿帮动一场手术。三位请看——
t_en: Don't get me wrong. I am not looking for bloodshed. Instead, I intend to perform surgery on the Synwish Syndicate. Look here...

t_ch: 靠着一些关系，我有幸得到了这张可以加入星神游戏的「欢愉假面」，有人对这宝贝趋之若鹜，也有人担心它是祸根。
t_en: Through certain connections, I secured this "Mask of Elation," the entry pass to the Aeonic game. Some covet this treasure, while others fear it a curse.

t_ch: 你们冒认「仙舟第一杀手」，应该是为它而来的吧？你们想争夺神明的奖赏，对吗？
t_en: Now, you claimed the name of "Xianzhou's Number One Assassins," so surely you came for this. You want the god's reward, don't you?

t_ch: 您是雇主，仪式的奖赏本该由您拿下才对。池波先生打算向星神索取什么？
t_en: You are our employer. Therefore, the festival's prize should rightfully be yours. What do you want from the Aeon, Mr. Polwave?

t_ch: 星神？我可没有那样不切实际的奢望。
t_en: The Aeon? I harbor no such unrealistic desire.

t_ch: 这份索取的机会，我把它当成给三位的报酬。我只想沾沾光——
t_en: I'll leave this prize for you to claim as your reward. I'm happy enough to share in a bit of your glory...

t_ch: 无论成败，我只希望几位被采访时广而告之，你们是由鸽川的「共愿帮」资助，这样就好。
t_en: Whether you win or lose, all I ask is that when the press comes knocking, you say this: You were sponsored by the Synwish Syndicate of Dovebrook. That is enough for me.

t_ch: 原来这是一场赞助生意？共愿帮是想要借此扬名？
t_en: So this is a sponsorship deal? The Synwish Syndicate only wants fame?

t_ch: 我们既然不是您雇佣的人，您不担心我们出尔反尔？无视您的要求？
t_en: Since we are not truly in your employ, are you not afraid we might break our promise and ignore your request?

t_ch: 你们真要对我的诉求漠不关心，又何必问呢？我刚才说过，本人虽不像仙舟人那样长寿，但好歹有些看人的眼光。
t_en: If you truly cared nothing for what I asked, why even raise the question? As I said before, I may not have the long life of the Xianzhou people, but I do know how to judge people.

t_ch: 容小女子再多问一嘴，您执着扬名，是为了什么？
t_en: One more question from me, please. Why are you so set on chasing fame?

t_ch: 在二相乐园，人们投注在幻造种身上的目光，对他们的好奇和诉求，就是令他们得以存在的愿力。
t_en: In Planarcadia, imagenae exist due to Wishpower, and that Wishpower comes from the attention, curiosity, and demands humans place upon these imagenae.

t_ch: 共愿帮的存在，最初是为了供养那些不被关注、没有愿力、也无人需要的幻造种。
t_en: The Synwish Syndicate was founded to provide for those imagenae who were ignored, had no Wishpower, and were not needed by humanity.

t_ch: 可惜，自打那个{RUBY_B#真珠}贝壳女士{RUBY_E#}上台后，我们打打杀杀的路要到头了。
t_en: Unfortunately, ever since that {RUBY_B#Pearl}seashell woman{RUBY_E#} rose to power, our path of blood and violence had run its course.

t_ch: 所以，我想让「共愿帮」换个形式存在，让它拥有比过去多得多的关注！
t_en: That's why I want the Synwish Syndicate to take on a new form, one that will draw more attention than ever before!

t_ch: 「共愿妖怪养老基金会」，你们觉得这个名字怎么样？
t_en: The Synwish Strangeling Pension Foundation. What do you think of the name?

t_ch: …是个温情脉脉的好名字。
t_en: ...It's a cozy and heartwarming name.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，那么，三位是否打算接下这单生意？
t_en: Hahaha! So, will the three of you accept this job?

t_ch: 片刻之后，鸽川区某处
t_en: A moment later, somewhere in Dovebrook District

t_ch: …这位池波先生的目标，倒是意外地脚踏实地。
t_en: ...Mr. Polwave's goal is surprisingly grounded.

t_ch: 能活到这把年纪的帮派首领，靠的不是保镖，而是生存智慧。
t_en: A gang leader won't survive this long by relying on bodyguards. It's about having a sharp instinct for survival.

t_ch: 他想让组织洗白，只怕也没那么容易。依小女子愚见，那位二当家就未必会和他的老大同心同德。
t_en: He wants to launder his gang clean, but that will not be easy. In my view, the underboss may not see eye to eye with his boss.

t_ch: 多谢二位的保护，我能有惊无险地离开。
t_en: My thanks to you both. With your protection, I managed to leave without harm.

t_ch: 只是，带走了本该属于「仙舟第一杀手」的面具…这么一想，这副面具都有些扎手了。
t_en: Yet, we carried off the mask meant for the so-called "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin"... when I think of it that way, this mask feels thorny in my hands.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#刃}那个男人{RUBY_E#}…想要加入幻月游戏？他想做什么？
t_en: {RUBY_B#Blade}That man{RUBY_E#}... He seeks to enter the Phantasmoon Games? What does he plan to do?

t_ch: 无论他为何而来，兹事体大，恐怕小女子得先失陪了，待到回禀爻光将军后再作决断。
t_en: Whatever his purpose, this matter is grave. I'm afraid I must take my leave for now and report to General Yao Guang before making any decisions.

t_ch: 不必了，我在此等各位已有一阵子了。
t_en: No need for that. I've been waiting here for you all for some time.

t_ch: 爻老板，你竟然亲自来了？
t_en: Madam Yao, you came in person?

t_ch: 这不，冒昧给你身后两位递上了假消息，致二位一番奔波，还是得老老实实来请罪啊。至于为何到此…停云姑娘，第三只锦囊，你怕是还没拆开吧？
t_en: Well, you see, I must admit I passed false information to the two behind you, sending them on a wild goose chase. I should personally ask for their forgiveness. As for why I am here... Miss Tingyun, you still have not opened the third pouch, have you?

t_ch: 这倒是……
t_en: That's true...

t_ch: 「无论对方给什么，照单全收带给我。˶╹ᵕ╹˶ಣ」
t_en: "Whatever they give you, just take it all and bring them to me. ˶╹ᵕ╹˶ಣ"

t_ch: 这是什么马后炮锦囊？
t_en: What kind of after-the-fact strategy is this?

t_ch: 将军，您若是开口讲明，我和星期日断不会拒绝。为何却要骗我们「使团有人迷途失陷」？
t_en: General, had you spoken plainly, Sunday and I would never have refused you. Why did you lie to us and claim that someone was "lost and captured"?

t_ch: 我可没有骗人。
t_en: I didn't lie.

t_ch: 若没有「前任龙尊」和「匹诺康尼在逃主公」两位现身，停云姑娘恐怕仍要困在那群莽夫无休止的试探中。「迷途失陷」，正是我卜测所见的未来。
t_en: If not for the appearance of the "former Vidyadhara high elder" and the "runaway master of Penacony," Miss Tingyun would still be trapped in those brutes' endless probing. Her being "lost and captured" was exactly the future I divined.

t_ch: 我还算到共愿帮要遭血光之灾，若此事成真，多半是因为武力纠缠间，停云姑娘没能留住力。而两位一来，共愿帮也保住了性命。
t_en: I also foresaw bloodshed awaiting the Synwish Syndicate. Had it come true, it would have been because Miss Tingyun failed to restrain her strength during the clash. Yet with you two here, the gangsters' lives were spared.

t_ch: 您果然算到了我身体的异状。
t_en: You even foresaw the anomaly with my body.

t_ch: 有时候纵然算到了命数，未必就能改得了。万幸，一切没往最糟的结果发展。
t_en: Sometimes, even when fate is foreseen, it cannot be changed. Fortunately, things did not come to the worst.

t_ch: 星期日先生，久仰大名！你在匹诺康尼的登神壮举，本座有幸借着{RUBY_B#观自在眼}千只翎眼{RUBY_E#}遥遥看了全程直播，堪称壮丽绝伦！
t_en: As for Mr. Sunday, I've heard so much about you. With my {RUBY_B#Eye of Īśvara}thousand feathered eyes{RUBY_E#}, I had the privilege of watching the broadcast of your entire god-ascending feat in Penacony from far away. Truly magnificent!

t_ch: 行差踏错的一步，将军还是不要再提了。
t_en: Let's not speak of that misstep anymore, general.

t_ch: 倒是我们如您所愿带回了面具。您打算如何用它？不只想单单握在手中，以绝星核猎手入局的心思吧。
t_en: Rather, we have brought back the mask as you asked. How do you intend to use it? Surely not just to hold it and block the Stellaron Hunter's entry?

t_ch: 本该如此，但是一因生百果，面具既然到了我手上，能有的花样多着呢。
t_en: That was the plan, but from one cause spring a hundred outcomes. Now that the mask is in my hands, there are many possibilities.

t_ch: 借它将刃诱来，合力围杀，你觉得如何？又或者——
t_en: What do you say about using it to lure Blade here and strike him down together? Or perhaps...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>本座亲自戴上这副假面，逐鹿「幻月游戏」，倒也有趣？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>I could wear the mask myself and personally compete in the Phantasmoon Games. That'd be interesting, right?</color>

t_ch: 这面具戴一副少一副，星核猎手也会为此头疼，哈哈，真是一举两得。
t_en: Each mask used is gone for good. The Stellaron Hunters will be left with a real headache. Hahaha, that'd be truly killing two birds with one stone.

t_ch: 爻老板，这可是要命的麻烦事，说笑不得呀。
t_en: Madam Yao, this is deadly business. It's no laughing matter.

t_ch: 「要命」…停云姑娘，本座正是为此而来的呢。
t_en: "Deadly"... Miss Tingyun, that is exactly why I am here.

t_ch: 当然，不是为了刃的命，他与仙舟的因缘多年来早就断得七七八八，倒也不必非得让本座截胡了别人的任命，为他前来此处。
t_en: Not to put Blade in his grave, that is. His ties to the Xianzhou have long since frayed. There was no need for me to snatch someone else's assignment and come here only for his sake.

t_ch: …截胡？
t_en: ...Snatch someone else's assignment?

t_ch: 所以仙舟派来的使节原本另有其人？
t_en: So the Xianzhou envoy was supposed to be someone else?

t_ch: 不瞒诸位，二相乐园本该由「神策将军」景元出使——罗浮云骑大战铁墓归来，这份荣耀本该归他。
t_en: To be frank, this journey to Planarcadia was meant for Jing Yuan, the Divine Foresight. The Luofu Cloud Knights recently returned from the great battle against Irontomb, and the honor should have been his.

t_ch: 只是临行前，我为此次出使所涉各方占上几卦，却得到如下结果——
t_en: Yet before his departure, I cast several divinations on all parties involved and received this result...

t_ch: 焚如，死如，弃如…小女子虽然不懂占卜，但能看得出来，这可不是什么吉兆。
t_en: Incineration. Death. Abandonment... I may not know divination, but even I can tell that there is no good omen.

t_ch: 很遗憾，玉阙和罗浮两位顶尖卜者，也就是我和我师妹符玄，彼此印证推演后，结论出奇地一致——
t_en: Unfortunately, the two top diviners of the Yuque and the Luofu — in other words, my junior soror Fu Xuan and I — cross-checked each other's extrapolations and came to the same astonishing conclusion...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>在这座「欢愉」的乐园中，会有一位帝弓天将陨落。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>An Arbiter General will fall in this arcadia of Elation.</color>

t_ch: 明知如此，你还要代替景元出使？
t_en: Even so, you're still taking the mission meant for Jing Yuan?

t_ch: 是，我向元帅进言，接了这份差事。没别的，只是本座从未见过这般离奇的命数，很有兴趣见证一番。
t_en: Yes. I contacted the Marshal and took over the task. I've simply never seen such a bizarre fate and wanted to witness it.

t_ch: 哪怕会因此而死？
t_en: Even if it means your death?

t_ch: 不过一死罢了。
t_en: It's only death.

t_ch: 若能在这场幻月游戏中为登神片刻而死，也不算辱没仙舟的声名。停云姑娘，把面具给我吧。
t_en: If I can die in these Phantasmoon Games to achieve a moment of godhood... Then my death won't have sullied the Xianzhou's honor. Miss Tingyun, hand me the mask.

t_ch: 这…抱歉，星期日，身体本能地察觉到了危险。
t_en: Umm... sorry, Sunday. My body instinctively sensed danger.

t_ch: 「加入幻月游戏的假面一旦被佩戴，便会与谒者本人紧密联结在一起——它会显现出佩戴者灵魂的形状。」我在《涯海星槎胜览》中看过类似的记述。
t_en: "Once the mask for joining the Phantasmoon Games is worn, it binds tightly to the Supplicant and manifests the shape of the wearer's soul." I read something like this in the Views of the Universe from a Starskiff.

t_ch: 如今验明正身，这幅面具确实是真品。
t_en: It's confirmed now. This mask is indeed genuine.

t_ch: 恭喜你啊，星期日先生，你现在已经是这堂堂星神游戏中的谒者了。
t_en: Congratulations, Mr. Sunday. You're now a Supplicant in the Aeonic game.

t_ch: 戎韬将军，你究竟想做什么？你两次贸然行事，是要与星穹列车交恶吗？
t_en: What exactly are you trying to do, Seer Strategist? Twice now you've acted without warning. Do you mean to make an enemy out of the Astral Express?

t_ch: 丹恒。
t_en: Dan Heng.

t_ch: 还是我来处理吧。
t_en: Let me take care of this.

t_ch: （星期日，你这是打算动手？）
t_en: (Sunday... are you planning to fight?)

t_ch: （不要担心。我当然不是这位将军的对手。）
t_en: (Don't worry. I'm no match for this general.)

t_ch: 爻光将军…你到底是如何窥探到了我的所思所想？我以「调律」检视过了，没有外力侵入的痕迹。
t_en: General Yao Guang... How'd you pierce my thoughts? I ran a check with my "tuning," and there were no signs of any outside intrusion.

t_ch: 都说卜者算不准人心，要我说，不过是些庸手的托辞——星期日先生，你对「幻月游戏」很感兴趣，甚至冒出了「若能戴上这副面具又会如何」的念头，对吧？
t_en: They say a diviner can't read hearts. I call that the excuse of mediocrity. Mr. Sunday, you're fascinated by the Phantasmoon Games. You even wondered "what if I wore this mask," didn't you?

t_ch: 这种念头，也是能靠卜卦算出来的？
t_en: You can even divine thoughts like that?

t_ch: 不能，我只是善用「眼睛」看。虽然星期日先生从未向你索要端详面具，但从刚才到现在，他的双眼至少三次看向这件奇物。
t_en: No. I'm simply good at seeing using my "eyes." Though Mr. Sunday never asked to examine the mask, he's glanced at it at least three times since we started the conversation.

t_ch: 以他这等人物的心性涵养，我实在想不出「感兴趣」之外的第二种解释。
t_en: Given the character and manners of someone as noble as him, I can't think of any motive for this action but "genuine interest."

t_ch: …星期日？
t_en: ...Sunday?

t_ch: 嗯，我确实有这样的想法，刚才的片刻，我甚至冒出了佩戴它的念头。
t_en: Yes. I did think of it. For a moment, I even considered putting it on.

t_ch: 所以，我只是将你想要的给了你。这可不是什么突然陷害，不过是给了你一份意想不到的「礼物」罢了——
t_en: So I merely gave you what you desired. This is no trap. It's just an unexpected "gift"...

t_ch: 幻月游戏进行期间，本座会提供一切力所能及的援手，助你夺得谒见星神的机会。
t_en: During the Phantasmoon Games, I'll give every ounce of aid I can to help you seize your chance to have an audience with the Aeon.

t_ch: 爻光将军…就我所知，先一步抛出重利，往往是为了诱人亏输。
t_en: General Yao Guang... As far as I know, throwing out heavy profits first is usually a lure to make others lose.

t_ch: 我们不过是初识，我想不明白，是什么让你愿意做到这一步？
t_en: We've only just met. I don't understand why you'd go this far?

t_ch: 何必妄自菲薄？<color=#dbc291ff>本座机关算尽，正是为了自「不可能」中凿开一线「可能」。</color>
t_en: Why belittle yourself? <color=#dbc291ff>Every move I've made is to carve a sliver of "possibility" from "the impossible."</color>

t_ch: 以我「观自在眼」所见，你是这片土地上，唯一不必为我做些什么，只要按自己的心意行事，就能带来变数的人。
t_en: With my Eye of Īśvara, I see you're the only one in this land who doesn't need to do a thing for me. Simply acting on your own will can bring change.

t_ch: 「自己的心意」……
t_en: "Your own will"...

t_ch: 嗯，丹恒，我确实想对这个世界了解得更深入一些。
t_en: Indeed. Dan Heng, I do want to learn more about this world.

t_ch: 因为这片「欢愉」的世界令我感到好奇，「二相乐园」…真的是乐园吗？
t_en: Because this world of Elation fascinates me. Is Planarcadia... truly a paradise? An arcadia that its name claims?

t_ch: 我曾为了建成乐园，抱着错误的愿景一意孤行，甚至对乐园应当是什么样貌，至今也无法笃定地给出回答。
t_en: I once pursued a mistaken vision of paradise with stubborn resolve. Even now, I can't say for sure what a true paradise should look like.

t_ch: 所以，我想亲自验证，这片「乐园」是否真如其所名？
t_en: That's why I want to see with my own eyes whether this "arcadia" lives up to its name.

t_ch: 这么说来，只要星期日先生行动起来，爻老板，你之前占卦所得的「死兆」……
t_en: So then, as long as Mr. Sunday acts, that "omen of death" Madam Yao foresaw...

t_ch: ——当然还是死定啦！难道你指望送出一份厚礼，就能随便逃出生天吗？
t_en: Of course it would still come to pass! Did you think just a lavish gift could let you stroll out of destiny's shackles alive?

t_ch: 容小女子多嘴一句，此事仙舟是否知情？
t_en: Pardon me for butting in... But does the Xianzhou know about this?

t_ch: 事关重大，我怎么会擅作主张？我和玉阙六御商议过了，若我有什么三长两短，我会推荐一位合适人选继任。
t_en: This is such serious business. I'd never make this decision on my own. I've talked it over with the Six Charioteers of the Yuque. If something happens to me, I'll line up a solid successor.

t_ch: 自我出使之日起，为防万一，她将前往玉阙，与玉阙六御所提的其他候选人一同在遍智格物院受训术数、兵法、治平之道。
t_en: She headed to the Yuque on the day I left on this mission, just in case anything went wrong. There, with the other potential successors picked by the Six Charioteers, she'll train at the Omniscience Sapientia Academe in divination arts, war strategies, and civil governance.

t_ch: 您是说符玄大人？她会接替您掌握玉阙符节？
t_en: You mean Lady Fu Xuan? She'd take over the Yuque after you?

t_ch: 符玄？什么符玄？
t_en: Fu Xuan? What Fu Xuan?

t_ch: 若是这趟旅程我未能归返，罗浮太卜司的青雀也许有望接过「戎韬将军」的符节，节制「玉阙」……
t_en: If I don't make it back from this trip, maybe Qingque from the Luofu Divination Commission could take the Seer Strategist's position and command the Yuque...

t_ch: ——青雀、玉阙，多衬啊！
t_en: ...Qingque and Yuque. What a perfectly rhymed match!

t_ch: 个中详情…三位，我们换个地方谈谈吧，如何？
t_en: As for the details... shall we discuss this somewhere else, you three?

t_ch: 一段时间后，为了寻回失窃物品，一位不速之客悄然潜入了鸽川区。
t_en: Some time later, in search of stolen items, an uninvited guest quietly slipped into Dovebrook District.

t_ch: 从地图上看，「不死神探事务所」就在这了。
t_en: Map says the "Ashen Detective Agency" should be right here.

t_ch: …但怎么只开着一家杂志社？
t_en: ...But why's there just a magazine publisher's office?

t_ch: 狸了个大谱！听说绘世学院那边出了个大新闻！
t_en: Holy furboly! Heard big drama hit Graphia Academy!

t_ch: 狸猫？在拍照？阿哈眷顾的土地上真是什么都有，去问问路吧。
t_en: A furbo? Taking photos? You really can find anything in the land blessed by Aha. Let's go ask for directions.

t_ch: 你好啊，小狸猫，你知道这附近有没有一家「不死神探事务所」？
t_en: Hey, little furbo. Know if there's an "Ashen Detective Agency" nearby?

t_ch: …不知道哩。但是，如果地上有一万信用点的话，可能会想起来喔？
t_en: ...Beats me. But if I find <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> credits on the ground, I might recall the spot?

t_ch: 问个路要收一万？算了…办正事要紧。
t_en: Ten thousand for directions? Hmmm... Fine, job first.

t_ch: 啊，天哪，我信用账户里的一万信用点不知怎么竟然跑到了你的账户里。现在，可以告诉我了吗？
t_en: Oh wow, looks like <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> credits from my account just hopped to end up in yours for no reason. Now, spill?

t_ch: 出手好大方的小姐！你要找的地方就在楼上喔。那位侦探借我们老板的地盘开办事务所。
t_en: Whoa, generous miss! The place you're looking for is upstairs. That detective's renting my boss's space for his agency.

t_ch: 但这家伙白天睡大觉，晚上大门紧锁。谁也不知道他靠什么生活！难道，他是吸血鬼吗？
t_en: But he's zonked out in the day, and his doors are locked at night. No clue how he earns any money. Maybe he's a vampire?

t_ch: 这不就是没开门吗？那，我该怎么见到这位不死神探呢？
t_en: So they're closed? Then how do I meet this Ashen Detective?

t_ch: …不知道哩。但是，如果地上有五万信用点的话，可能会想起来喔？
t_en: ...Beats me. But if I find <unbreak>50,000</unbreak> credits on the ground, I might recall that too?

t_ch: 臭狸猫，你丫真是欠收拾啊，竟敢诈骗咱们的客人！
t_en: You stinky furbo, begging for a smack, scamming our guest!

t_ch: 溜了溜了！
t_en: Time to go!

t_ch: 你好，我是「旁白」，是「不死神探事务所」的接待员，也是那位侦探先生的助手。
t_en: Hi, I'm "Narrator," receptionist at the Ashen Detective Agency, and the assistant of that Mr. Detective.

t_ch: 「旁白」？谁家好人会给自己的宠物起这么个名字啊？
t_en: "Narrator"? What sane person gives their pet that name?

t_ch: 楼上那位就是了。对了，你没被那只臭狸猫骗了钱吧？
t_en: That would be the dude upstairs. By the way, that mangy furbo didn't fleece you, right?

t_ch: 骗了，足足一万呢。
t_en: It did. A full <unbreak>10,000</unbreak>.

t_ch: 要不要我替你撵上那只臭狸猫，把钱要回来。
t_en: Do you want me to catch that sneaky furbo for you and get your money back?

t_ch: 就不麻烦你了…好了，钱回来了。连带还有那家伙信用点户头里剩下的二十六块五毛余额。
t_en: Nah, save your breath... Got it fixed. Money's back in my account, plus the last <unbreak>26.5</unbreak> credits from that guy's account balance.

t_ch: 不错嘛，朋克洛德的黑客。
t_en: Not bad, Punklordian hacker.

t_ch: 大叔，你还蛮有见识的。那么，可以麻烦你带个路吗？
t_en: You seem pretty knowledgeable. So, would you mind showing me the way?

t_ch: 让您见笑，还得走条「小道」。杂志社的房东躲债逃走了，债主轮番上门轰炸，侦探先生干脆把正门给封死了。
t_en: Sorry, but you will have to take a "side path." The landlord of this magazine office fled to dodge his debts, and creditors have been storming in one after another. In the end, Mr. Detective sealed the front door entirely.

t_ch: 所以，侦探先生就住在这儿？
t_en: So this is where the detective lives?

t_ch: 没错，办公居住一体化，确实寒碜了点。这个点，他应该在睡大觉吧，我们这儿也难得来一次客人。
t_en: Yes, his office and living quarters are one and the same. It is a little shabby, I admit. At this hour... he is probably fast asleep. Visitors here are rare indeed.

t_ch: 难得来一次客人…听起来真叫人悲伤啊。
t_en: Visitors being rare... that really does sound a little sad.

t_ch: 您跟我来，我这就把他叫醒。
t_en: Please, come with me. I will wake him at once.

t_ch: 先办正事吧，晚点再闲逛。
t_en: Let's get down to business. I can stroll around later.

t_ch: 不死途先生，这位小姐并不是来送外卖的。
t_en: Mr. Ashveil, this lady is not here for food delivery.

t_ch: 哦，那就是来杀我的吧？
t_en: Oh, then she must be here to kill me?

t_ch: 很遗憾，也不是。
t_en: Sadly, not that either.

t_ch: 到底遗憾在哪里……
t_en: What do you mean, "sadly"...?

t_ch: 不是送外卖的，也不是来杀我的。真是怪了…噢！难道是，委托人！你是来委托我查案的！
t_en: Not here to deliver food, AND not here to kill me. How strange... Oh! Then you must be a client! You are here to hire me for a case!

t_ch: 你的猜测顺序实在是令人有些心疼。没错，我是来委托你查案的，有东西失窃了。
t_en: Your order of guesses is a little heartbreaking. Yes, I am here to commission you. Something has been stolen.

t_ch: 哦，你丢了什么东西呢？
t_en: Oh? What did you lose?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>丢了人，连带着我本该拿到手的一张古老面具。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>A person, along with an ancient mask that should have been mine.</color>

t_ch: 哼哼，有点意思。你是来加入「幻月游戏」的？
t_en: Heh, interesting. Are you here to join the Phantasmoon Games?

t_ch: 此刻，侦探的内心升起了一团疑云——她到底是什么来头？
t_en: At that moment, a cloud of suspicion rises in the detective's mind. Who exactly is this girl?

t_ch: 老白，谈生意的时候就不要配音了。
t_en: Mister N, stop the voiceover when we're talking business.

t_ch: 哎？要停下来吗？你付我时薪九百信用点外加两根香蕉不就是干这个的吗？
t_en: Huh? Stop? You pay me nine hundred credits an hour plus two bananas just for this, don't you?

t_ch: 对，但干活的时候，多少给我提几个词。侦探和助手，两个人中好歹得有一个懂点推理吧？
t_en: Yes, but at least throw me a few clues while you work. Between a detective and their assistant, at least one of them should know a thing or two about deduction, right?

t_ch: 一个名字突然在侦探先生那空空如也的脑袋里升起。这个表面看来不起眼的女孩，却是名列公司通缉榜赏金前十的恐怖分子，星核猎手——
t_en: A name flashed through the otherwise empty mind of the detective. This unremarkable-looking girl was in fact one of the top ten most wanted terrorists on the IPC's bounty list, a Stellaron Hunter...

t_ch: ——卡芙卡！
t_en: ...Kafka!

t_ch: 是银狼，她是银狼啊！不死途先生，你的脑袋让冰箱门给挤了吗？
t_en: It's Silver Wolf! She's Silver Wolf! Mr. Ashveil, did you get your head caught in the fridge door or something?

t_ch: 我感觉需要再销毁一遍自己的信息了。
t_en: I feel like I need to wipe my personal data again.

t_ch: 星际和平公司悬赏金额最高的通缉犯，跑来委托一个穷困潦倒的侦探——这类故事我倒是看过不少。
t_en: One of the Interastral Peace Corporation's most wanted fugitives with the highest bounties, comes to commission a broke detective... I have seen plenty of stories like this.

t_ch: 下一步，我就该身不由己地被卷入一场大阴谋里，开始逃亡，对吧？
t_en: So, the next thing would be me getting dragged into a massive conspiracy and forced to run for my life, right?

t_ch: 别被害妄想了。我来找你，是因为我挖进治安局的刑事档案记录系统看了看。
t_en: Drop the paranoia. I came to you because I hacked into the Security Bureau's criminal records system.

t_ch: 不查不知道，一查吓一跳，那些警方奇案怪案的资料中，你被聘用的次数可是多得很呢。
t_en: What I found was shocking. In all those bizarre police cases, you were hired again and again.

t_ch: 「同谐教团圣餐奇案」「幽灵疾锋龙列车案」「幽灵连续复活事件」「奇笑馆超不连续密室杀人案」…都是你帮忙解决的。
t_en: "The Curious Case of the Harmony Sect Communion," "The Mystery of the Ghost Bullet Wyrm Express," "The Serial Ghost Resurrection Incident," "The Super-Non-Serial Locked-Room Murders at Kooky House"... you helped solve them all.

t_ch: 你不是最有名的，但你应该是这里最有能耐的侦探，对吗？
t_en: You may not be the most famous, but you're probably the most capable detective around here, right?

t_ch: 还有没有点隐私啊…算了，说说看，面具是怎么丢的。
t_en: Is nothing private anymore... Whatever. Tell me, how did you lose your mask?

t_ch: 今天本该是和共愿帮碰头的日子……
t_en: Today was supposed to be our meeting with the Synwish Syndicate...

t_ch: 好的，那么接下来谈谈委托费的问题吧。
t_en: Alright, now let's talk about the commission fee.

t_ch: 拜托，我一句话都还没说完……？
t_en: Excuse me? I haven't even finished my sentence yet...?

t_ch: 真正的名侦探，即便足不出户也能对案情了如指掌。
t_en: A true great detective can know a case without ever leaving the room.

t_ch: 他睡着的时候，我会负责搜罗情报。关于共愿帮今天的遭遇，还有那三个骗走面具的「仙舟第一杀手」，我们已经大致了解情况了。
t_en: While he sleeps, I gather the intel. We already have the basics about today's fiasco with the Synwish Syndicate, and those three "Xianzhou's Number One Assassins" who scammed the mask.

t_ch: 能搞定吗？钱不是问题。对了，你们收黑钱吗？
t_en: Can you handle it? Money is not a problem. Do you take dirty money?

t_ch: 不好意思，不收黑钱。也请您务必不要在付款后将报酬连同账户余额一同撤回。
t_en: Sorry, no dirty money. And please do not withdraw your payment plus the account balance after paying.

t_ch: 哦，那这样吧，这儿不是公司的地盘吗？你把我扭送领赏吧。
t_en: Oh, then how about this? This is the IPC's turf, right? Turn me in for the bounty.

t_ch: 你拿完赏金我就跑路。星核猎手偶尔也会用这招赚点经费。
t_en: You take the reward, and I bolt afterward. We Stellaron Hunters sometimes use that trick to raise funds.

t_ch: …要不然我们还是谈谈收黑钱的可能性吧。三千万信用点，事后给就行。
t_en: ...Or maybe we could discuss the possibility of taking dirty money. Thirty million credits. Pay us after the work's done.

t_ch: 成交，那我这几天努努力。这是我的联系方式，你们有消息了随时告诉我。
t_en: Deal. I'll try to get that money over the next few days. Here's my contact info. Update me the moment you have any news.

t_ch: 事后给？咱们事务所什么时候有这个规矩了？
t_en: Pay us after the work? When did our office have a rule like that?

t_ch: 就在刚才。老白啊老白，难道你还真想收下一笔黑钱？我想到了另一个来钱的路子。让我先联系一下某位大人物。
t_en: That rule got made just now. Oh, my dear Mister N, did you truly want to take dirty money? I have come up with another way to make money. Let me reach out to a certain big shot first.

t_ch: 真珠女士，我得到了一个可靠的消息！
t_en: Madam Pearl, I've obtained solid intel!

t_ch: …不死途先生？你会在这时候主动来电，概率可不到5％。
t_en: ...Mr. Ashveil? The chance of you initiating a call at this hour is under five percent.

t_ch: 推测：也许你愿意考虑我的<color=#dbc291ff>提议</color>了？
t_en: Let me guess: Perhaps you are finally ready to consider my <color=#dbc291ff>offer</color>?

t_ch: 不，我发现了星核猎手的踪迹！他们现在就在二相乐园，甚至还准备加入「幻月游戏」搅局！
t_en: No, I've found the trail of the Stellaron Hunters! They are here in Planarcadia, and they even plan to crash the Phantasmoon Games!

t_ch: 我可以把她的坐标给你，神不知鬼不觉……
t_en: I can send you her coordinates quietly, without anyone noticing...

t_ch: 嗯哼，神不知鬼不觉。
t_en: Hmph. Quietly, without anyone noticing.

t_ch: 「幻月游戏」…打从贵公司决定速通开始，它就注定不只是一局逗闷子的游戏了。您希望我加入，实在是有些虐待老人了吧。
t_en: The Phantasmoon Games... The moment your IPC chose to speedrun it, it was fated to be more than just a game of fun. And you want me to take part? That is asking a bit much of an old man.

t_ch: 早知道刚才就应该答应那个黑客的要求，把她扭送领赏才对啊。
t_en: We should've taken that hacker's deal earlier and turned her in for the bounty.

t_ch: 意外之喜。真珠还藏着其他面具啊…那就好办多了。
t_en: An unexpected bonus. So Pearl still has other masks hidden away... That makes things much easier.

t_ch: 看来谈完生意顺道窃听一下合作伙伴这个好习惯…还得保持。
t_en: Seems that listening in on one's partners after wrapping up business is a habit worth keeping.

t_ch: 喂，阿刃，在哪？
t_en: Hey Bladie, where are you?

t_ch: 面具找到了？
t_en: Did you find the mask?

t_ch: 还没。不过我有了新线索。丢了一张面具，我再找一张不就是了。
t_en: Not yet. But I've got a new lead. We might've lost one mask, so I just need to find another.

t_ch: 好。答应我，下次别再用什么「仙舟第一杀手」的名目在暗网上接活了，好吗？
t_en: Fine. Promise me this: Next time, don't take contracts on the dark web using names like "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin."

t_ch: 好好好。对了，你能告诉我吗，这次艾利欧给了你什么剧本？
t_en: Okay, okay. By the way, can you tell me what script Elio gave you this time?

t_ch: 一片空白。
t_en: A completely blank page.

t_ch: （该办正事了。共愿帮盘踞在哪里呢？）
t_en: (Time to get down to business. Where could the Synwish Syndicate be hiding?)

t_ch: （共愿帮这边的接头人，应该很显眼才是。）
t_en: (The contact from the Syndicate should stand out.)

t_ch: 那边几个人…也是共愿帮的成员？
t_en: Those people over there... are they also members of the Synwish Syndicate?

t_ch: 共愿帮的总部就在河对岸，够气派吧？
t_en: That's the Synwish Syndicate headquarters across the river. Quite impressive, don't you think?

t_ch: 您请跟我来。
t_en: Please follow me.

t_ch: 这是…画中通道？
t_en: Is this... the passage in the painting?

t_ch: 当然，目前被咱们共愿帮征用中！
t_en: Of course. The Synwish Syndicate has it under our command for now!

t_ch: 二当家您回来了！二当家您辛苦了！
t_en: Underboss, you are back. Thanks for your hard work!

t_ch: 这狐狸是谁？
t_en: Who's this fox?

t_ch: 欢迎二当家回家！
t_en: Welcome home, Underboss!

t_ch: 这位是老大请来的人？
t_en: Is this the one the boss brought in?

t_ch: （对这班不知轻重的莽夫，恐怕我得收着点力了……）
t_en: (Looks like I'll have to hold back against these reckless brutes...)

t_ch: 不愧是怖•鬼人！好大的力气！
t_en: As expected of The Terror in Fog! Such incredible strength!

t_ch: 体内那股「毁灭」的力量…还是难以驾驭…
t_en: That power of the Destruction within me... it's still hard to control...

t_ch: 爻老板，我已如你所说，竭尽所能让他们别死了。
t_en: Madam Yao, I've done everything in my power to keep them alive, as you said.

t_ch: 我不想取你性命，你自觉退下吧。
t_en: I don't want to take your life. Back down while you still can.

t_ch: 共愿帮的男儿何惧死亡！！别小瞧我的气概！！
t_en: A true warrior of the Synwish Syndicate fears no death!!! Don't underestimate my resolve!!!

t_ch: 「第一杀手」们，你们要去哪儿？老大在楼上等着呢！
t_en: "Number One Assassins," where are you going? The boss is waiting upstairs!

t_ch: 看不出来，这儿装修得颇有品味。
t_en: I gotta say, this place is decorated with a touch of style.

t_ch: 暴力组织不是总得展示暴力的，更多时候，他们需要展示财力。
t_en: A gang does not always flaunt brute force. More often, they need to flaunt wealth.

t_ch: 黑客小姐，请这边走。
t_en: This way please, Miss Hacker.

t_ch: 还有射击键？有点意思。
t_en: So there is even a shooting button? That's interesting.

t_ch: 怎么这么多…狸猫？
t_en: Why are there so many... furbos?

t_ch: 都是这间杂志社的员工。
t_en: They're all employees of this magazine company.

t_ch: 这画面怎么像游戏里的惩罚环节...…
t_en: Why does this scene feel like a punishment round from a game...

t_ch: 职场求生，可比游戏残酷多了。
t_en: Office survival's way more brutal than any game.

t_ch: 真是出人意料……
t_en: Now that is... astonishing.

t_ch: 哇哦，好大的机器人。
t_en: Wow! That's one massive robot!

t_ch: 诸位，欢迎莅临珠星的总部大楼。
t_en: Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to the headquarters of the Pearluxe Corp.

t_ch: 姬子姐说这是家综合娱乐公司，当时咱就在想——那它的总部可不能是座普普通通的写字楼啊。
t_en: Back when Himeko said this was an entertainment company, I knew their headquarters wouldn't be some ordinary old office building.

t_ch: 但，这也太有特色了吧！超大的机器人雕像，这完全就是一座超级机库嘛！
t_en: But this is way too much! That giant robot statue... This place is totally like some supersized mecha hangar!

t_ch: 哈哈，您说得一点没错！按创始人的要求，敝公司本部的大厅被特意修建成了如今的样貌。
t_en: Haha, you're absolutely right! In accordance with the vision of our founder, our lobby was intentionally constructed as you see it now.

t_ch: 为消费者实现超越期待的梦想，这正是珠星一向坚守的设计宗旨。如果各位中有熟悉珠星作品的观众，应该能认出，这是——
t_en: To realize dreams that far exceed what consumers imagine has always been Pearluxe Corp's core design philosophy, and we have never strayed from it. If any of you are familiar with our company's works, you may recognize this as—

t_ch: 「机铠战士系列」，《破晓战队》的行动基地。
t_en: "Mechatron Warrior Series," the Daybreak Squadron's base of operations.

t_ch: 啊哈哈，看来杨先生是本公司动画的资深爱好者啊。
t_en: Ahaha, looks like Mr. Yang here is quite a fan of our company's animated works!

t_ch: 星穹列车的各位，<color=#dbc291ff>在正式会谈开始前，真珠女士希望各位能在这里游览体验一番，她已派出专人作陪，还请贵宾们稍作等待。</color>
t_en: Now, <color=#dbc291ff>Madam Pearl would like to invite everyone from the Astral Express to take a tour before your official meeting begins. She has arranged for a member of our staff to accompany you, so please wait here a moment.</color>

t_ch: 我明白了，费伦先生，有劳您一路的迎接。
t_en: I understand, Mr. Feiron. Thank you for escorting us all this way.

t_ch: 那么，我先失陪了。侦探先生，这边请。
t_en: Well then, I'll take my leave. This way, Detective.

t_ch: 有缘再见啦，各位。
t_en: See you around, everyone!

t_ch: 喔？真珠在会谈前希望我们先在这儿逛逛？还真是奇怪的安排……
t_en: Really? Pearl wants us to look around here before the meeting? That seems kinda weird...

t_ch: 这会和她提出的合作有关吗？我很好奇。也许这是她对自己另一重身份的「自我介绍」。
t_en: Could it be related to her proposed collaboration? Perhaps this is her way of "introducing" her other identity.

t_ch: 不管怎么样，有逛逛，一切就好！趁接待的人还没来，咱们四下看看？
t_en: It's all good as long as we get a chance to look around, that works for me! Why don't we explore while we wait for our new guide?

t_ch: 杨叔，这不是你最喜欢的大、机、器、人嘛！
t_en: Mr. Yang, isn't this your favorite gi-ant ro-bot!

t_ch: 大、机、器、人！
t_en: GI-ANT RO-BOT!

t_ch: 造、物、引、擎！
t_en: EN-GINE OF CRE-A-TION!

t_ch: 怎么看都不是造物引擎吧！
t_en: There's no way that's an Engine of Creation!

t_ch: 机铠，屹立于大地之上！
t_en: Behold the mechatron, standing tall astride the earth!

t_ch: 咱词穷了，总之就一个字，太帅了——！
t_en: I don't know what to say... Except: THIS! IS! AWESOME!

t_ch: 我看到了看到了，别那么大声。我们是来做客的，在外面还是得保持仪态庄重。
t_en: I saw it, please, just keep it down. We're here as their guests, we should be careful about what sort of impression we leave.

t_ch: 仔细一看，这台机铠的许多部件都没能安装。
t_en: Looking more closely, it appears many of this mechatron's components were never installed.

t_ch: 真是遗憾啊，好想看看拼装完成的机铠，动起来的它会是何等英姿！
t_en: What a shame! I was hoping to see it finished, to see the completed mechatron! Imagine how it would look in motion! What a glorious sight that would be!

t_ch: 杨叔，快跟咱讲讲，这台大机器人和你做动画师时设计的那个…「阿拉哈托」，到底哪个更厉害啊？
t_en: Really? In that case, Mr. Yang, let's hear it: Which one's stronger, this giant robot or that "Arahato" you designed back when you were an animator?

t_ch: 这是在人家的地头，跨作品比较机器人的战力未免对创作者们有些失礼了吧。
t_en: Seeing as we're on someone else's property, comparing robots from different works would be most disrespectful to the creators themselves.

t_ch: 说说嘛，我也很好奇！
t_en: C'mon, tell us! Pretty please? I wanna know too!

t_ch: 人类嘛，不比较会死喔。
t_en: Comparing things is human nature! We can't help ourselves!

t_ch: 是啊，我看《银河战力党》的玩家们总会把各个角色的战斗力比来比去的。
t_en: Yeah! Like how Cosmicon Collective players are always obsessed with powerscaling all the characters!

t_ch: 阿拉哈托是机器人动画，破晓战队是真人英雄剧…这两者真没法比较。
t_en: Arahato is an animation all about robots, while Daybreak Squadron is a live action hero drama... The two really cannot be compared.

t_ch: 好标准的成年人式回答啊。
t_en: What a stereotypically textbook "mature" answer.

t_ch: 唉，你们就放过我这个已经不再热血的成年人吧。难得来这么有趣的地方，小三月，{NICKNAME}，咱们三个人一起在这巨大的机铠下合个影吧？
t_en: My days of being a hotheaded young man are well behind me, so please, cut this "mature" old man some slack, would you? We don't usually come to a place this cool, so why don't the three of us take a photo under this massive mechatron?

t_ch: 好耶，让我设个自动拍摄，这样咱们就能一块儿照进去了。
t_en: Now we're talking! Let me set the self-timer, so we can all get in the shot together.

t_ch: 好耶。
t_en: Booyeah!

t_ch: 记得开美颜。
t_en: Don't forget to turn on the beauty filter!

t_ch: 为什么照片里突然多了一个人？
t_en: Where did this extra person in the photo come from?

t_ch: 大白天的，怎么还能在人气这么旺的地方闹鬼呢？
t_en: A ghost? But it's daytime! And there's way too many people here anyway!

t_ch: 见鬼了！
t_en: Ghastly!

t_ch: 这又是谁啊？
t_en: Who's this now?

t_ch: 你谁啊！
t_en: Who are you!?

t_ch: 喔！唔？啊……嗯！
t_en: Oh! Huh? Ah... Mm!

t_ch: 真是…好伟大的一张脸啊！
t_en: Wow, this truly is... a magnificent face!

t_ch: 和新闻上看到时完全不一样，能近距离端详，简直有种不真实的感觉。
t_en: It's nothing like how it looks on the news... Being able to see it up close feels almost surreal.

t_ch: 靠太近了？对不住，怪我没有把握好距离感。
t_en: Am I too close? Sorry, my bad, I'm not great with personal space.

t_ch: 会夸就多夸几句。
t_en: Keep the compliments coming!

t_ch: 嘿嘿～打量这么伟大的脸，这种冲动完全是不可抗力啊，你就原谅我吧。
t_en: Hehe~ Sorry, sorry! It's impossible not to stare at a face this amazing! You'll forgive me, right?

t_ch: 再靠近我就要报警了。
t_en: Any closer and I'm calling the cops.

t_ch: 嘿嘿～打量这么伟大的脸，这种冲动完全是不可抗力啊，你就原谅我吧。
t_en: Hehe~ Sorry, sorry! It's impossible not to stare at a face this amazing! You'll forgive me, right?

t_ch: 下次别吓我了，不然你赔不起。
t_en: Don't scare me like that again. You can't afford the consequences.

t_ch: 哎，那你可能要失望了，因为我真的没有钱。
t_en: *sigh* You're probably gonna be disappointed then, 'cause I'm totally broke.

t_ch: 哎呀，忘了向各位出示了，这是我的工牌。我是珠星财团幻造技术研发部专员，虚照，在星际和平公司的职级是P<unbreak>30</unbreak>。
t_en: Oh right, I forgot to show you my ID. Right, here we are! I'm Nihilux, and I work at Pearluxe Corp's Imagenesis Technology Department. I'm a P<unbreak>30</unbreak> agent who works for the IPC.

t_ch: 虚照小姐，你好。
t_en: Hello, Miss Nihilux.

t_ch: P<unbreak>30</unbreak>是多高的职级？
t_en: How high up the corporate ladder is P<unbreak>30</unbreak>?

t_ch: 稍微高级一些的牛马罢了。和诸位平时往来的战投部大人物们肯定不能比。
t_en: Just a slightly higher-ranking workhorse, that's all. Not on the same level as those big shots from the Strategic Investment Department who you folks usually deal with.

t_ch: 所以，您有何贵干？
t_en: So, what brings you here?

t_ch: 啊！是真珠女士派我来大厅迎接列车的贵客的。
t_en: Ah! Madam Pearl sent me to the lobby to pick you up!

t_ch: 诸位都是有名的超•银河级英雄，脸在新闻上刷了不止一次，我一眼就认出来了！所以迫不及待地闪过来想和诸位合照一张，嘿嘿，见谅见谅！
t_en: All of you are famous super-cosmic-level heroes whose faces have been all over the news more times than I can count, so of course I recognized you right away and just couldn't help rushing over here to snap a selfie with you! Hehe, hope you don't mind!

t_ch: 原来如此，你好啊，虚照小姐。
t_en: I see. Then hello, Miss Nihilux.

t_ch: 各位好！接下来将由我担任导游，引领大家全方位、深入这座大楼的各处区域打卡游览。
t_en: Hello everyone! Starting now, I'll be your tour guide, taking you on a comprehensive and in-depth tour to every single must-visit place in this building!

t_ch: 无论是会让瓦尔特先生感兴趣的机铠幻造，还是三月小姐关心的《苍天航路绒绒号》的动画产品，将由我来为各位揭晓这些大热产品背后的秘密！
t_en: From the mechatron imagenesis that managed to pique Mr. Yang's interest, to Miss March's favorite Fluffy Across the Blue merchandise, I'll reveal all the secrets behind our hottest products!

t_ch: 真的吗？我都有点迫不及待了！
t_en: Seriously!? I can't wait!

t_ch: 当然是真的，一路上各位有什么想看的，想了解的，甚至想要的，全都可以告诉我！
t_en: Of course I'm being serious! If there's anything you want to see, learn about, or even get your hands on along the way, anything at all, just let me know!

t_ch: 杨先生，你对这座机铠雕像恋恋不舍，是想多拍几张留念合照吗？
t_en: Mr. Yang, you seem reluctant to part with the mechatron. Want to take a few more pictures with it?

t_ch: 我只是好奇…这台巨大的机器人在多久之后会完工？
t_en: I'm just curious... when will this giant robot be completed?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，小小剧透一下，这其实也是今天参观流程的一环喔，<color=#dbc291ff>我们想邀请列车英雄参与这座机铠雕像的装配。</color>
t_en: Hehe, here's a little spoiler: That's actually part of today's tour! <color=#dbc291ff>We'd like to invite the heroes of the Astral Express to help complete this mechatron statue.</color>

t_ch: 那它在完成装配后，能像二相乐园的其他机铠那样行动吗？
t_en: Will it be able to move like the other mechatrons in Planarcadia once it's done?

t_ch: 这恐怕…就有些难度了。各位应该听说过吧？机铠是由愿力驱动的幻造种，内部并没有什么复杂机械结构。
t_en: That's... I'm afraid it'll be a bit tricky. You're probably all aware that mechatrons are imagenae driven by Wishpower, which means they don't actually have any complex machinery inside them.

t_ch: 人嘛，看着没有生命的外物时，总会不自觉将它想像成有生命，可以思考和活动的存在。就是这种「移情作用」产生的愿力赋予了机铠——不，赋予了所有幻造种以生命。
t_en: You know how humans tend to unconsciously imagine inanimate objects as living things that can think and move? It's this empathetic projection that grants Wishpower to mechatrons, no, to all imagenae, giving them life.

t_ch: 那这台大机铠怎么就不行了？
t_en: So what's wrong with this giant mechatron, then?

t_ch: 俺寻思，这大机铠也能动啊？
t_en: I'm thinkin' this big ol' bot oughta be able to move too, yeah?

t_ch: 光你一个人寻思当然是不行滴。越大的幻造种，就需要越庞大的愿力维持。
t_en: Think away, but one person's thoughts alone won't cut it. The bigger the imagenae, the more Wishpower needed to sustain it.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>要让这样巨大的幻造种活过来，必须跨越「不可能存在」的思维桎梏才行。这就需要承载巨大愿望的故事和不受拘束的想象力。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>We'd need to break free from the mental shackles of "impossibility" to bring such a massive imagenae to life. That in turn requires stories filled with the grandest of wishes and imaginations that know no bounds.</color>

t_ch: 可惜呀，现代人类的思维太现实啦。人们总会忍不住去想，这么大的机铠，到底是怎么动起来的？这么巨大的重量，它为什么没被压垮呢？
t_en: Alas! Modern humans think way too practically. People can't help but wonder: "How does such a huge mechatron even move? Given how massive it is, why doesn't it just collapse under its own weight?"

t_ch: 只消掺杂一点点怀疑，巨型机铠屹立于大地之上，就是个遥不可及的梦。也许，它要是出现在原始人社会里倒是更容易活过来吧。
t_en: The tiniest seed of doubt, and the giant mechatron, standing tall astride the earth becomes nothing but an impossibility. Maybe it'd have a better chance of coming to life in a more primitive society.

t_ch: …还真是令人遗憾啊。
t_en: Truly, a pity.

t_ch: 高兴点嘛，您今天有机会将这台巨大机铠拼装完成——这可是多少机器人爱好者们求之不得的一刻啊。
t_en: Cheer up! Today's your chance to finish building this massive mechatron! Think of how many robot enthusiasts would kill for this kind of opportunity!

t_ch: 有句俗话说得好啊：发财梦，英雄心，千般努力归珠星。
t_en: There's a saying that goes: Dreams of wealth, the heart of a hero, and countless efforts all lead to Pearluxe Corp.

t_ch: 不管是小孩还是大人，大家总会做些美梦：成为从邪恶势力的手中拯救世界的英雄！与巨大的机铠合体放出酷炫的大招！珠星出售这些梦想，然后从中大赚特赚。
t_en: Whether you're a kid or an adult, everyone has their own sweet dreams: Becoming a hero who saves the world from evil forces! Merging with a giant mechatron to unleash awesome ultimate moves! Pearluxe Corp sells these dreams and makes a killing off of them.

t_ch: 诸位看，这个区域销售珠星生产的机铠模玩，其中销量最好的还是「破晓战队」系列。
t_en: If I could have everyone's attention, you can buy models of Pearluxe Corp's mechatrons in this area, among them, is the best-selling "Daybreak Squadron" series.

t_ch: 哇，这个玩具…看起来好精巧，难怪杨叔会对这些机铠念念不忘。
t_en: Wow, look at this... it's so intricate! No wonder Mr. Yang's got mechatrons on the brain.

t_ch: 咳咳，真羡慕二相乐园的孩子啊。我成长的年代里，机器人玩具还是稀罕东西。
t_en: I very much envy the kids here in Planarcadia. Back in my day, robot toys were still quite the luxury.

t_ch: 哈哈，看着几位眼放光的样子，我不禁想起，自己也曾经年轻过。
t_en: Haha, seeing all your eyes light up like that reminded me that I too was once young.

t_ch: 那时候的我，有一阵子穷得就连去便利店里买盒牛奶都要犹豫半天。我在心里默默告诉自己，我一定要努力奋斗，一定可以大发横财的。
t_en: Back then, there was a stretch when I was so broke that I'd spend ages debating whether to buy a carton of milk from the convenience store. I kept telling myself that I had to work hard so I could get rich someday.

t_ch: 那…有钱的目标，您实现了吗？
t_en: Soooo... Did you manage to get rich?

t_ch: 经过多年的努力和打拼，姐姐我呀，终于也不再年轻了。
t_en: After years of hard work and hustling, at the very least, I'm no longer young.

t_ch: 您不但年轻，还很美丽。
t_en: You are both young and beautiful.

t_ch: 呜呜，小嘴儿真甜。不过姐姐有自己保持年轻的秘诀……
t_en: Aww, aren't you the sweetest! But I do have my secret to staying young...

t_ch: 那就是谎报年龄，你也多学着点吧。咳咳，说回努力和打拼……
t_en: That is, lying about your actual age. Keep that in mind, just in case! *ahem* Now, back to the grind...

t_ch: 您也属于珠星了。
t_en: You also ended up in the hands of Pearluxe Corp.

t_ch: 怎么可能！悄咪咪地告诉你们，其实我还有别的身份和兼职。
t_en: That's not true! Let me tell you a secret: I actually have other identities and side gigs.

t_ch: 喔？难道您除了珠星的工作之外另有产业？
t_en: Oh? Don't tell me you have another business venture outside your job at Pearluxe Corp?

t_ch: 欸嘿嘿…也不算什么产业，一笔小买卖罢了。
t_en: Hehe... it's not really a business, just a small side hustle.

t_ch: 大发横财不敢说，但小赚一笔的目标，姐姐我就快要做到咯。
t_en: I wouldn't call it striking it rich, but I'm pretty close to making a sweet little profit.

t_ch: 别吊咱们胃口啦，虚照小姐，快展开讲讲？
t_en: Don't keep us in suspense, Miss Nihilux! Just spill the beans, pretty please?

t_ch: 说出来怕你们不信，我有几位朋友，他们一直在试图创造一种去中心化的数字货币来对抗公司的信用点体系。
t_en: You might not believe this, but I have some friends who've been trying to create a decentralized digital currency to fight against the IPC's credit system.

t_ch: 这些人莫非来自朋克——
t_en: Are these people from Punklorde—

t_ch: 没错，这些人来自「游牧矿工」这个派系，他们发现了可以盗取博识尊算力「挖矿」的方法，正在挖掘一种总量有限的「博识币」。姐姐我已经拿全部身家重仓了这个项目！
t_en: That's right, these people are from the Nomadic Miners faction. They've discovered a way to hijack Nous' computing power for mining and are digging up a limited supply of Learned Coins. And I have gone all-in with everything I got on this project!

t_ch: 总觉得…这种行为的风险不小。
t_en: I feel like... this is a very risky way of doing things.

t_ch: 哈哈，人生嘛，起起落落是常事。风险越高，收益越大！
t_en: Haha, life's full of ups and downs, right? The higher the risk, the bigger the reward!
